definitions: A logical error where one mistakes an abstract concept for a concrete reality.
The mistake of reifying an abstraction, treating it as if it were a tangible entity.
A philosophical error in which an abstract idea is treated as if it has independent existence or causal power.
examples: The Abstraction Fallacy often leads to flawed arguments.
He was accused of committing the Abstraction Fallacy.
Understanding the Abstraction Fallacy is crucial in critical thinking.
